Designed for two, discover the serene beauty of San Francisco’s waterways with a self-guided kayaking experience at either Lake Merced or China Basin. Participants also learn the rules for headfirst entries and begin to learn to enter the water headfirst from a seated position at poolside (if the water is 9 feet deep or deeper). On successful completion of Level 3, participants have achieved basic water competency in a pool environment. Prerequisite: Successful completion of Level 2. Children must be 48 to participate.

Level 4 - This class should improve participants' proficiency in performing the swimming strokes that were introduced in Level 3. Participants perform these strokes with increased proficiency and can swim them for greater distances. In addition, participants learn the arm actions that accompany the scissors kick and breaststroke kick to begin performing the sidestroke and breaststroke at rudimentary proficiency levels. Participants also learn the back crawl and butterfly at rudimentary proficiency levels, in addition to the basics of performing a simple open turn at a wall. Prerequisite: Successful completion of Level 3.

Level 1 This class is designed to orient participants to the aquatic environment and to help them gain basic aquatic skills. In addition, participants start learning about how to be safe around water. Children must be 48 to participate.

The Maunupau    Method-Learning & Fun Tennis System is a curriculum based progressive    learning methodology whose platform begins with a technically themed lecture,    drills to support the lecture, & fun games. Students can expect to learn    the etymology of grips; continental, eastern, semi-western, &    western(full), stances; square, closed, & open,  the difference between topspin &    underspin, pronation on the serve, ground strokes & volleys, match play,    & the art of the rally. Campers will also learn SAT vocabulary words, a    tennis themed art project, & chess. Tennis is a high cerebral process    game & all of the other activities of the camp aide in this decision    making process.  Campers are never    under any pressure to perform, only to learn, & to conduct themselves    with sportspersonship on court.

We    offer a safe place for beginning skateboarders to learn how to stand on a    skateboard properly while maintaining our balance. Campers enjoy a fun and active    summer filled with games, sports, swimming, arts and crafts, and exciting    field trips. Playtime to follow storytime.

Level 3 - This class builds on the skills learned in Levels 1 and 2. Participants learn to swim the front crawl and elementary backstroke at rudimentary proficiency levels and are introduced to the scissors and dolphin kicks. Participants learn the survival float and increase the time duration for treading water. Participants also learn the rules for headfirst entries and begin to learn to enter the water headfirst from a seated position at poolside (if the water is 9 feet deep or deeper). On successful completion of Level 3, participants have achieved basic water competency in a pool environment. Prerequisite: Successful completion of Level 2. Children must be 48 to participate.

Designed for two, discover the serene beauty of San Francisco’s waterways with a self-guided kayaking experience at either Lake Merced or China Basin. Paddle at your own pace as you explore the lake’s tranquil waters, diverse wildlife, and natural habitats; an ideal way to connect with nature and enjoy a refreshing outdoor adventure right in the city. Enrollment includes the rental of a tandem (two-person) kayak and covers up to two participants. Personal flotation devices are provided for all participants. Children 12 and under must be accompanied by an adult in the kayak. All participants must sign a release waiver on the day of their session. Instructional safety videos must be viewed prior to paddling; a link to the videos and waiver will be emailed the week before your scheduled time. Registered participants under the age of 12 must be accompanied on the water by a registered parent or guardian. Expect to get wet—please bring a towel and a change of clothes.

event_name: City Outdoor Rec Expedition - Girls - Session 1
event_description: Adventure awaits—starting in San Francisco’s parks and ending beneath the stars at Camp Mather.  Over two memorable weeks, campers join CORE (City Outdoor Rec Expedition) for Outdoor Rec favorites like rock climbing, archery, kayaking, and exploring everything the city has to offer—all while learning the basics of tent setup, outdoor living, and caring for the environments they explore. Then the journey continues to Camp Mather, where campers trade city sights for mountain air, lakeside fun, and classic camp traditions—campfires, hiking, ropes course challenges, and more. We’ll travel by bus on Monday and spend 4 nights under the starts, returning on Friday. This fully outdoor camp encourages participants to grow in confidence, build new friendships, and deepen their appreciation for nature—whether they’re adventuring close to home or miles from the city. A mandatory pre-camp meeting will be held prior to the start of Week 2 to review packing lists, logistics, and expectations for the overnight week at Camp Mather.

The Maunupau    Method-Learning & Fun Tennis System is a curriculum based progressive    learning methodology whose platform begins with a technically themed lecture,    drills to support the lecture, & fun games. Students can expect to learn    the etymology of grips; continental, eastern, semi-western, &    western(full), stances; square, closed, & open,  the difference between topspin &    underspin, pronation on the serve, ground strokes & volleys, match play,    & the art of the rally. Campers will also learn SAT vocabulary words, a    tennis themed art project, & chess. Tennis is a high cerebral process    game & all of the other activities of the camp aide in this decision    making process.  Campers are never    under any pressure to perform, only to learn, & to conduct themselves    with sportspersonship on court.

Knotting    isn’t just for pearls, it’s a way to keep precious gemstone beads evenly    spaced, prevent wear at the edges, and save the beads from scattering if the    thread is broken. Try this ancient technique for yourself! This class will    discuss the differences between thread material and types of gemstones.    You’ll learn how to properly thread precious gemstones, including pearls,    into a necklace or bracelet. You’ll have access to a variety of different    types of clasps, thread, and gemstones so you can truly create a    one-of-a-kind piece of wearable art! Tools and materials available. Light    refreshments provided to quench your thirst as you work.

This    class is a playful, low-pressure dive into abstract image-making through hand    printed techniques. We’ll focus on core visual principles: texture,    repetition, color, and composition while creating intuitive, expressive    prints using linoleum blocks, gelatin plates, and collage. With zero pressure    to “draw something” or create representational images, students will build    visual fundamentals organically, making it a great fit for beginners or rusty    creatives. Emphasizing process, intuition, and play, this class encourages    flow-state work that’s restorative and creatively satisfying. Each student    will develop a mini-series of prints or a visual journal exploring their own    personal visual language. Please bring required materials to class. Materials    list available at sharonartstudio.org.

event_description: Level 3 - This class builds on the skills learned in Levels 1 and 2. Participants learn to swim the front crawl and elementary backstroke at rudimentary proficiency levels and are introduced to the scissors and dolphin kicks. Participants learn the survival float and increase the time duration for treading water. Participants also learn the rules for headfirst entries and begin to learn to enter the water headfirst from a seated position at poolside (if the water is 9 feet deep or deeper). On successful completion of Level 3, participants have achieved basic water competency in a pool environment. Prerequisite: Successful completion of Level 2. Children must be 48 to participate.

The    Maunupau Method/Learning & Fun Tennis System(LAFTS) curriculum uses a    progressive learning module to teach entry level students to play tennis with    proper technique. Students can expect to learn grips, swing flight paths,    stances, the art of the rally, shoulders being perpendicular to the net,    & match play. This is not a beginner's class.

There is much more in Hatha yoga practice than poses. In this series, we still explore practices aimed to improve our physical well-being through asanas (poses); we also introduce practices that include pranayama (breath expansion) techniques, and finally we experience the benefits of simple meditation techniques. The intention is awareness at a physical, mental and emotional level on as well as off the mat.

An Introduction to skateboarding. Learn the basics of skateboarding from balance, pushing, turning and cruising on low impact ramps and surfaces. We offer a safe place for beginning skateboarders to learn how to stand on a skateboard properly while maintaining our balance. We will cover the safety aspects and “flow” of skating flat ground obstacles/ramps. We’ll also assist in basic skateboard maintenance and identify each part of the skateboard. Our instructors are trained and experienced in teaching youth and helping kids and adults navigate their way through the fundamentals of skateboarding. Helmets are mandatory. We will provide helmets and skateboards if needed.

The Maunupau    Method-Learning & Fun Tennis System is a curriculum based progressive    learning methodology whose platform begins with a technically themed lecture,    drills to support the lecture, & fun games. Students can expect to learn    the etymology of grips; continental, eastern, semi-western, &    western(full), stances; square, closed, & open,  the difference between topspin &    underspin, pronation on the serve, ground strokes & volleys, match play,    & the art of the rally. Campers will also learn SAT vocabulary words, a    tennis themed art project, & chess. Tennis is a high cerebral process    game & all of the other activities of the camp aide in this decision    making process.  Campers are never    under any pressure to perform, only to learn, & to conduct themselves    with sportspersonship on court.

Our after school program is an experience that provides a safe and nurturing environment for youth in grades K-5 to develop life long recreation and leisure skills. Participants will receive homework assistance, be exposed to an environment that will promote literacy, encourage communication and empowerment skills while providing recreation activities that include sports, group games, arts and culture, community exploration and seasonal special events. Parents will have the option to pay the entire fee upon registration. Payment plans are available. $270 is due upon registration and on the first day of October, January and March of the school year.

Level 4 - This class should improve participants' proficiency in performing the swimming strokes that were introduced in Level 3. Karate originated in the Okinawa Islands of Japan and has branched out into many different styles. Karate Kidz is a structured class that teaches formalized, sequenced movements which represent offensive and defensive postures known as Kata. Students will also learn kicking, punching, blocking and other open hand techniques. Students will have the opportunity to purchase a uniform and belts for an extra fee.

This class builds on the skills learned in Levels 1 and 2. Participants learn to swim the front crawl and elementary backstroke at rudimentary proficiency levels and are introduced to the scissors and dolphin kicks. Participants learn the survival float and increase the time duration for treading water. Participants also learn the rules for headfirst entries and begin to learn to enter the water headfirst from a seated position at poolside (if the water is 9 feet deep or deeper). On successful completion of Level 3, participants have achieved basic water competency in a pool environment. Prerequisite: Successful completion of Level 2. Children must be 48 to participate. Join us    for a one-day workshop exploring one of the oldest photographic processes,    the cyanotype. This alternative process produces a characteristic blue hued    photographic print. In this workshop participants will use found objects like    flowers, plants, or other common items to create unique photograms using the    cyanotype process. We will cover the basics of the process from mixing and    applying emulsion to exposure and development. Participants are encouraged to    bring their own objects with them on the day. Advice will be provided on    suitable objects.

There is much more in Hatha yoga practice than poses. In this series, we still explore practices aimed to improve our physical well-being through asanas (poses); we also introduce practices that include pranayama (breath expansion) techniques, and finally we experience the benefits of simple meditation techniques. The intention is awareness at a physical, mental and emotional level on as well as off the mat.

Designed for two, discover the serene beauty of San Francisco’s waterways with a self-guided kayaking experience at either Lake Merced or China Basin. Paddle at your own pace as you explore the lake’s tranquil waters, diverse wildlife, and natural habitats; an ideal way to connect with nature and enjoy a refreshing outdoor adventure right in the city. Enrollment includes the rental of a tandem (two-person) kayak and covers up to two participants. Personal flotation devices are provided for all participants. Children 12 and under must be accompanied by an adult in the kayak. All participants must sign a release waiver on the day of their session. Instructional safety videos must be viewed prior to paddling; a link to the videos and waiver will be emailed the week before your scheduled time. Registered participants under the age of 12 must be accompanied on the water by a registered parent or guardian. Expect to get wet—please bring a towel and a change of clothes.

This class introduces hand building techniques including pinch, coil, and slab construction, and basic wheel throwing techniques of wedging, centering, throwing cups and bowls, and trimming. Learn to  feel more confident with your portrait drawing by examining     the anatomy, structure, and proportion of the head, neck, and shoulders of  the figure. We will work both     from live models and from our imagination and use a variety of materials  including charcoal, graphite,     and pastel, colored pencil. After this class you will feel more confident  in your ability to not only draw     portraits with greater facility but also and with a more unique dynamic  energy. Discover    the colorful, creative world of polymer clay jewelry!      In this hands-on class, instructor Sophie Wertz will guide you through the    process of designing and creating your own unique sets of earrings. Polymer    clay has become a favorite medium among DIY jewelry makers — it’s    lightweight, vibrant, and incredibly versatile. Once you learn a few key    sculpting techniques, the design possibilities are endless! You’ll start by    getting familiar with polymer clay through short lessons on color mixing,    clay conditioning, and handling techniques. Sophie will demonstrate popular    methods like marbling, sculpting miniature florals, and using carving and    modeling tools. You’ll have access to a variety of cutters, rollers, and    texture tools to shape and personalize your designs. From there, you’ll learn    how to prepare clay sheets, bake your creations, assemble, and finish your    earrings. By the end of class, you’ll go home with a custom jewelry piece and    leave with confidence, skills, and know-how to keep creating polymer clay    jewelry on your own! Tools and materials provided.

** The times for this camp have been updated to 1pm-3pm** This    half-day camp meets Monday through Thursday for 3-weeks and is for campers    interested in exploring techniques in jewelry and metal arts. Both beginning    and intermediate level students are welcome. Beginning students will be    introduced to the basic skills of professional jewelry making and    metalsmithing, and returning students will dive deeper into techniques to    create unique pieces such as pendants, earrings and rings as well as 3-D    objects made of copper and brass. Each camp ends with an art show for campers    to share their work with family and friends on the last Thursday of the    session. Previous experience is not required, but projects require the    ability to focus and follow multi-step procedures. Attendance is mandatory    each day and important to the completion of artwork. Materials are included    in the price of the camp.

Ready… set… explore the city you thought you knew! In this fast-paced, challenge-packed adventure, fully supervised teams of teens race across San Francisco using public transit, street maps, and sharp teamwork to uncover clues hidden at recreation centers throughout the city. Every day brings a new mission—mini-games, mystery puzzles, sports challenges, art tasks, community activities, and neighborhood photo quests—each designed to spark creativity, curiosity, and collaboration. Whether you’re athletic, artistic, brainy, or boldly adventurous, there’s a challenge waiting for you. The week builds toward an epic, staff-guided final race across the city, where campers put their navigation skills, problem-solving, and team spirit to the ultimate test!

Discover    the mesmerizing art of marbling in this hands-on workshop. Participants will    explore traditional and contemporary marbling techniques, learning how to    create flowing, one-of-a-kind patterns on paper and fabric using paints and    specialized tools. Guided step by step, you’ll experiment with color,    movement, and composition while gaining an understanding of the materials and    processes behind this centuries-old craft. No prior experience is    necessary—just curiosity and a willingness to play. The workshop offers a    relaxed, creative environment where you can slow down, enjoy the meditative    rhythm of marbling, and leave with several finished pieces to take home.    Perfect for artists, crafters, or anyone looking for a unique and inspiring    creative experience.

event_description: Adventure awaits—starting in San Francisco’s parks and ending beneath the stars at Camp Mather.  Over two memorable weeks, campers join CORE (City Outdoor Rec Expedition) for Outdoor Rec favorites like rock climbing, archery, kayaking, and exploring everything the city has to offer—all while learning the basics of tent setup, outdoor living, and caring for the environments they explore. Then the journey continues to Camp Mather, where campers trade city sights for mountain air, lakeside fun, and classic camp traditions—campfires, hiking, ropes course challenges, and more. We’ll travel by bus on Monday and spend 4 nights under the starts, returning on Friday. This fully outdoor camp encourages participants to grow in confidence, build new friendships, and deepen their appreciation for nature—whether they’re adventuring close to home or miles from the city. A mandatory pre-camp meeting will be held prior to the start of Week 2 to review packing lists, logistics, and expectations for the overnight week at Camp Mather.

The Maunupau    Method-Learning & Fun Tennis System is a curriculum based progressive    learning methodology whose platform begins with a technically themed lecture,    drills to support the lecture, & fun games. Students can expect to learn    the etymology of grips; continental, eastern, semi-western, &    western(full), stances; square, closed, & open,  the difference between topspin &    underspin, pronation on the serve, ground strokes & volleys, match play,    & the art of the rally. Campers will also learn SAT vocabulary words, a    tennis themed art project, & chess. Tennis is a high cerebral process    game & all of the other activities of the camp aide in this decision    making process.  Campers are never    under any pressure to perform, only to learn, & to conduct themselves    with sportspersonship on court.

During    this class students will learn how to make luminograms. Lumen prints are made    using gelatin silver paper, which is exposed to light and placed directly    into the fixer. The prints can be made using digital negatives or objects    (like a photogram), but they can also be made using only light itself.       Beautiful pastel tones emerge in the process, and the prints can be left    unfixed to further change over time. Students will experiment with different    techniques to better understand the factors that influence the colors of the    gelatin silver paper - the time of exposure, the type of light, and the kind    of paper used in the process.

Swim Academy Level 3 is for participants who have passed the Level 2 swim class. To move to the next level, swimmers must successfully pass the required exit skills. Enrollment in the next level is only offered in the following session, and only to those who meet the exit criteria—no exceptions. If a child does not pass their current level, they will not continue in the Academy at this time.

Level 3 - This class builds on the skills learned in Levels 1 and 2. Participants learn to swim the front crawl and elementary backstroke at rudimentary proficiency levels and are introduced to the scissors and dolphin kicks. Participants learn the survival float and increase the time duration for treading water. Participants also learn the rules for headfirst entries and begin to learn to enter the water headfirst from a seated position at poolside (if the water is 9 feet deep or deeper). On successful completion of Level 3, participants have achieved basic water competency in a pool environment. Prerequisite: Successful completion of Level 2. Children must be 48 to participate.

Level 4 - This class should improve participants' proficiency in performing the swimming strokes that were introduced in Level 3. Participants perform these strokes with increased proficiency and can swim them for greater distances. In addition, participants learn the arm actions that accompany the scissors kick and breaststroke kick to begin performing the sidestroke and breaststroke at rudimentary proficiency levels. Participants also learn the back crawl and butterfly at rudimentary proficiency levels, in addition to the basics of performing a simple open turn at a wall. Prerequisite: Successful completion of Level 3.

Level 4 - This class should improve participants' proficiency in performing the swimming strokes that were introduced in Level 3. Participants perform these strokes with increased proficiency and can swim them for greater distances. In addition, participants learn the arm actions that accompany the scissors kick and breaststroke kick to begin performing the sidestroke and breaststroke at rudimentary proficiency levels. Participants also learn the back crawl and butterfly at rudimentary proficiency levels, in addition to the basics of performing a simple open turn at a wall. Prerequisite: Successful completion of Level 3.

Level 3 - This class builds on the skills learned in Levels 1 and 2. Participants learn to swim the front crawl and elementary backstroke at rudimentary proficiency levels and are introduced to the scissors and dolphin kicks. Participants learn the survival float and increase the time duration for treading water. Participants also learn the rules for headfirst entries and begin to learn to enter the water headfirst from a seated position at poolside (if the water is 9 feet deep or deeper). On successful completion of Level 3, participants have achieved basic water competency in a pool environment. Prerequisite: Successful completion of Level 2. Children must be 48 to participate.

This    class introduces hand building techniques including pinch, coil, and slab    construction, and basic wheel throwing techniques of wedging, centering,    throwing cups and bowls, and trimming. Clay is available for purchase at the studio with a    100-pound clay limit per session. No outside clay or glazes allowed. Students    must purchase a basic pottery tool kit prior to the first day of class.    Suggested materials list can be found at sharonartstudio.org. Students may    enroll in only one ceramics class per session. Note: New students (new to    ceramics or Sharon Art Studio) must attend the first three classes. All skill    levels welcome.

This    half-day camp meets Monday through Thursday for 3-weeks and is for campers    interested in fine art! This camp gives young artists the opportunity to    experiment with different media and make friends among other art fans of    their age. Campers will explore a variety of techniques in drawing, painting,    and mixed media (such as collage, assemblage, and printmaking). They will    also explore outdoor drawing and plein air painting techniques nearby in    beautiful Golden Gate Park. Each camp ends with an art show for campers to    share their work with family and friends on the last Thursday of the session.    Previous experience is not required, but projects require the ability to    focus and follow multi-step procedures. Attendance is mandatory each day and    important to the completion of artwork. event_name: Mixed Media: Abstract and Figure-Hybrid Art
event_description: This    class emphasizes the power of inner vision—creating images from within,    rather than simply copying nature. While we will begin by studying and    imitating existing forms, the focus will shift toward transforming those    observations into original, expressive creations. The main goal of this    course is for students to discover their own unique visual style. By    exploring Surrealist, Cubist, and imitating ancient artistic influences,    students will experience the joy of creative transformation within oneself.      Students will first learn the principles of abstract geometric composition,    then move toward combining figurative or organic elements with abstraction.    The aim is to develop an individual artistic “flavor”—a step toward personal    originality.      Hands-on support will be provided throughout. No prior experience is    necessary. A $138 Model fee included in the price of the class. Please bring    supplies to class. Materials list available at sharonartstudio.org.

Each camp ends with an art show for campers to    share their work with family and friends on the last Thursday of the session. event_name: Woodworking in Small Spaces: Portable Workbenches!
event_description: Are you    interested in woodworking but like most city residents have limited space?    Have you built saw horses at the Randall and want to explore how to put them    to use? In this hands-on class you'll build a portable workbench that can    mount on those saw horses or at your kitchen table. From the classic hand    tools needed to make furniture (chisels, hand planes, measuring and marking    tools, and various types of saws) to small, portable power tools, you'll go    home with a deeper sense of what's possible in small spaces. Instructor Mike    Bray has decades of experience and scores of tricks and techniques for    working safely and accurately. A $100 materials fee is included in the cost    and covers the unique demands of the bench, including a bench vise, two    layers of finished ply, and hardwood framing. Prerequisite: Woodshop Safety    Orientation. Students who have not met the prerequisite will be withdrawn and    charged a 20% refund processing fee.

Learn    the organic metal art of lost wax casting. This course will cover the process    by which metal object is cast from a wax model, allowing you to see the    limitless forms that metal can take. A San Francisco tradition, Silver Tree Day Camp is located in Glen Park Canyon. Silver Tree is a 1-week camp for kids who enjoy the outdoors, being in nature, and don’t mind getting dirty. Activities include hikes and nature activities, games, campfire safety, arts and crafts, legends storytelling and rock climbing. Camp hours are from 9am-3:30pm, except for Thursdays. Each Thursday, families are invited to a performance of songs and skits from 4pm-5pm. On Thursdays, campers also enjoy a campfire lunch that includes hot dogs and s'mores. Extended care hours are included in the camp fee from 8:30am-9:00am for drop off. Pick up is no later than 5:30pm. Parents will need to walk to the Silver Tree building every day for pick up (about a 10 minute walk into the Canyon).

Pine Lake Day Camp is located in the west end of Stern Grove. Pine Lake is a 1-week camp for kids who enjoy the outdoors, being in nature, and don’t mind getting dirty. Activities include hikes and nature activities, games, field trips, arts and crafts and archery. Each Thursday, families are invited to a performance of songs and skits from 4pm-5pm. On Thursdays, campers also enjoy a campfire lunch that includes hot dogs and s’mores.

The Maunupau    Method-Learning & Fun Tennis System is a curriculum based progressive    learning methodology whose platform begins with a technically themed lecture,    drills to support the lecture, & fun games. Students can expect to learn    the etymology of grips; continental, eastern, semi-western, &    western(full), stances; square, closed, & open,  the difference between topspin &    underspin, pronation on the serve, ground strokes & volleys, match play,    & the art of the rally. Campers will also learn SAT vocabulary words, a    tennis themed art project, & chess. Tennis is a high cerebral process    game & all of the other activities of the camp aide in this decision    making process.  Campers are never    under any pressure to perform, only to learn, & to conduct themselves    with sportspersonship on court.

Level 3 - This class builds on the skills learned in Levels 1 and 2. Participants learn to swim the front crawl and elementary backstroke at rudimentary proficiency levels and are introduced to the scissors and dolphin kicks. Participants learn the survival float and increase the time duration for treading water. Participants also learn the rules for headfirst entries and begin to learn to enter the water headfirst from a seated position at poolside (if the water is 9 feet deep or deeper). On successful completion of Level 3, participants have achieved basic water competency in a pool environment. Prerequisite: Successful completion of Level 2. Children must be 48 to participate.

Our after school program is an experience that provides a safe and nurturing environment for youth in grades K-5 to develop life long recreation and leisure skills. Participants will receive homework assistance, be exposed to an environment that will promote literacy, encourage communication and empowerment skills while providing recreation activities that include sports, group games, arts and culture, community exploration and seasonal special events. Payment plans are available. $270 is due upon registration and on the first day of October, January and March of the school year.

The Maunupau    Method-Learning & Fun Tennis System is a curriculum based progressive    learning methodology whose platform begins with a technically themed lecture,    drills to support the lecture, & fun games. Students can expect to learn    the etymology of grips; continental, eastern, semi-western, &    western(full), stances; square, closed, & open,  the difference between topspin &    underspin, pronation on the serve, ground strokes & volleys, match play,    & the art of the rally. Campers will also learn SAT vocabulary words, a    tennis themed art project, & chess. Tennis is a high cerebral process    game & all of the other activities of the camp aide in this decision    making process.  Campers are never    under any pressure to perform, only to learn, & to conduct themselves    with sportspersonship on court.

A San Francisco tradition, Silver Tree Day Camp is located in Glen Park Canyon. Silver Tree is a 1-week camp for kids who enjoy the outdoors, being in nature, and don’t mind getting dirty. Activities include hikes and nature activities, games, campfire safety, arts and crafts, legends storytelling and rock climbing. Camp hours are from 9am-3:30pm, except for Thursdays. Each Thursday, families are invited to a performance of songs and skits from 4pm-5pm. On Thursdays, campers also enjoy a campfire lunch that includes hot dogs and s'mores. Extended care hours are included in the camp fee from 8:30am-9:00am for drop off. Pick up is no later than 5:30pm. Parents will need to walk to the Silver Tree building every day for pick up (about a 10 minute walk into the Canyon).

Discover the creative possibilities of gelli plate printing in this hands-on workshop designed for adult learners of all experience levels. Students will learn the basics of gelli printing, including how to prep the gelli plate, how to ink it, how to create designs that can be easily printed, and how to make stellar prints. We will begin with focused instruction in design and color specific to gelli printing, helping you understand how layering, contrast, and transparency work in this unique process. Moving forward, students will create images that work in a positive/negative style on their gelli plates, exploring how shapes, textures, and stencils interact to produce dynamic, one-of-a-kind prints. Participants will leave with a collection of original prints and a deeper understanding of this playful and versatile printmaking technique.

In addition, Pilates    increases circulation and helps to sculpt the body and strengthen the body's    core or powerhouse (torso). People who do Pilates regularly feel they have    better posture, are less prone to injury, and experience better overall    health.

Our after school program is an experience that provides a safe and nurturing environment for youth in grades K-5 to develop life long recreation and leisure skills. Participants will receive homework assistance, be exposed to an environment that will promote literacy, encourage communication and empowerment skills while providing recreation activities that include sports, group games, arts and culture, community exploration and seasonal special events. All staff and volunteers receive background checks and are trained in safety skills. Start times may vary based on the school release time. The fee for the school year is $1080. Parents will have the option to pay the entire fee upon registration. Payment plans are available. $270.00 is due upon registration and on the first day of October, January and March of the school year.

CORE: Adventure awaits—starting in San Francisco’s parks and ending beneath the stars at Camp Mather.  Over two memorable weeks, campers join CORE (City Outdoor Rec Expedition) for Outdoor Rec favorites like rock climbing, archery, kayaking, and exploring everything the city has to offer—all while learning the basics of tent setup, outdoor living, and caring for the environments they explore. Then the journey continues to Camp Mather, where campers trade city sights for mountain air, lakeside fun, and classic camp traditions—campfires, hiking, ropes course challenges, and more. We’ll travel by bus on Monday and spend 4 nights under the starts, returning on Friday. This fully outdoor camp encourages participants to grow in confidence, build new friendships, and deepen their appreciation for nature—whether they’re adventuring close to home or miles from the city. A mandatory pre-camp meeting will be held prior to the start of Week 2 to review packing lists, logistics, and expectations for the overnight week at Camp Mather.

SLICE is a community-focused program for teens and transition-aged youth with Autism or other developmental disabilities, ages 15–25. Designed to support growing independence, SLICE offers opportunities for young adults to engage in meaningful community service, develop professional and life skills, and participate in collaborative projects with other TR camps. Participants practice social skills, team building, and community integration through hands-on activities and local field trips, including those planned with the Everybody Travels Camp.  Due to a low staff ratio, participants must be independent with personal care, able to stay with the group with verbal prompting, and prepared for walking and travel during outings. For more information, call 415-831-6841 or email RPDTR@sfgov.org.

event_description: Colored pencils are versatile and precise. Enhance your drawing repertoire with gem-like sticks of color! In this four-week class, we'll start with exercises to create our own color charts that explore blending, alternate finishes, and texture. Next we will sketch from a basic still-life. Then we'll work on an exquisite rendering from a landscape photo for the last two meetings. Photo references will be provided, but instead you may certainly work from a picture that you have at home. There will be time for critiques and sharing our work with the class at the end of each session. Please bring materials to class. Materials list available at sharonartstudio.org.

event_name: Oil Painting Workshop: Trees of San Francisco
event_description: In this workshop, students will learn how to paint with oil in a non-toxic/sustainable way, while gaining a newappreciation for the trees in San Francisco - who will be serving as our muse for the class activity. We’ll do anoverview of oil paints as a medium, their history, and a quick overview of color theory. Then diving right in,students will be guided on how to use paint brushes and a palette knife to create their own unique version of a treeof San Francisco (provided by the teacher). This workshop is perfect for both beginners and intermediate artists who want to learn the basics of oil painting, sharpen their eye for detail, all while focusing on one of the magnificent trees of San Francisco. By the end of the workshop, students will have a new set of oil painting skills and the proof to show for it! Their ready to frame piece of art will make for a great gift or home decor addition! 8x10 canvas, palettes, palette knives, and cleaning materials provided. Students will be required to bring paint set and brushes, and be encouraged to bring an apron or wear clothes to get messy in. Please see materials list for what is required to bring to the workshop. Materials list available at sharonartstudio.org.

This    after school jewelry class is offered for those interested in taking their    metal arts skills to the next level. Both beginning and intermediate level    students are welcome. Beginning students will be introduced to the basic    skills of professional jewelry making and metalsmithing, and returning    students will dive deeper into techniques to create unique pieces such as    pendants, earrings and rings as well as 3-D objects made of copper and brass.    Intermediate students will be given the opportunity to work with sterling    silver. Materials are included in the price of the class. The Jewelry Studio    is located on the upper floor, with stair access only. For accommodations    requests or accessibility questions, please contact rpdaccess@sfgov.org or    415-831-6841. Please note that submitting your request at least two weeks    before the beginning of the class will help ensure availability.

Challenge yourself at your own pace! The course features high elements designed to help participants stretch beyond their comfort zones, all while safely harnessed and belayed by our trained staff. Every moment is an opportunity for discovery and lasting memories. Adults are welcome! Helmets and harnesses are provided.

This introductory class is designed for high school students, who wish to learn how to shoot, process and print film in the Photo Center darkroom. This course fills quickly. Instructor will email materials list prior to first class. Repeating this course is not recommended.

There is much more in Hatha yoga practice than poses. In this series, we still explore practices aimed to improve our physical well-being through asanas (poses); we also introduce practices that include pranayama (breath expansion) techniques, and finally we experience the benefits of simple meditation techniques. The intention is awareness at a physical, mental and emotional level on as well as off the mat.

event_name: Aquatics - Learn to Swim - Rossi Pool
event_description: Level 1 This class is designed to orient participants to the aquatic environment and to help them gain basic aquatic skills. In addition, participants start learning about how to be safe around water. Children must be 48 to participate.Level 2 This class builds on the basic aquatic skills and water safety skills and concepts learned in level 1. Participants begin to perform skills at a slightly more advanced level and begin gaining rudimentary propulsive skills on both the front and the back. This level marks the beginning of independent aquatic locomotion skills. Prerequisite- Completion of Level 1. Children must be 48 to participate.Level 3 This class builds on the skills learned in Levels 1 and 2. Participants learn to swim the front crawl and elementary backstroke at rudimentary proficiency levels and are introduced to the scissors and dolphin kicks. Participants learn the survival float and increase the time duration for treading water. Participants also learn the rules for headfirst entries and begin to learn to enter the water headfirst from a seated position at poolside (if the water is 9 feet deep or deeper). On successful completion of Level 3, participants have achieved basic water competency in a pool environment. Prerequisite: Successful completion of Level 2. Children must be 48 to participate.Level 4 This class should improve participants' proficiency in performing the swimming strokes that were introduced in Level 3. Participants perform these strokes with increased proficiency and can swim them for greater distances. In addition, participants learn the arm actions that accompany the scissors kick and breaststroke kick to begin performing the sidestroke and breaststroke at rudimentary proficiency levels. Participants also learn the back crawl and butterfly at rudimentary proficiency levels, in addition to the basics of performing a simple open turn at a wall. Prerequisite: Successful completion of Level 3.Level 5 This class focuses on helping participants refine their performance of all six swimming strokes (front crawl, back crawl, butterfly, breaststroke, elementary backstroke, and sidestroke). Participants perform these strokes with increased proficiency and can swim them for greater distances. Participants also learn to perform flip turns on the front and back. Prerequisite: Successful completion of Level 4.Registration is limited to two sessions per person.

This class is designed to help participants gain basic aquatic skills and swimming strokes, including the front crawl, breaststroke and elementary backstroke. Participants also learn skills and concepts needed to stay safe around water, in addition to those needed to help themselves or others in an aquatic emergency.

event_name: City Outdoor Rec Expedition - Boys - Session 2
event_description: Adventure awaits—starting in San Francisco’s parks and ending beneath the stars at Camp Mather.  Over two memorable weeks, campers join CORE (City Outdoor Rec Expedition) for Outdoor Rec favorites like rock climbing, archery, kayaking, and exploring everything the city has to offer—all while learning the basics of tent setup, outdoor living, and caring for the environments they explore. Then the journey continues to Camp Mather, where campers trade city sights for mountain air, lakeside fun, and classic camp traditions—campfires, hiking, ropes course challenges, and more. We’ll travel by bus on Monday and spend 4 nights under the starts, returning on Friday. This fully outdoor camp encourages participants to grow in confidence, build new friendships, and deepen their appreciation for nature—whether they’re adventuring close to home or miles from the city. A mandatory pre-camp meeting will be held prior to the start of Week 2 to review packing lists, logistics, and expectations for the overnight week at Camp Mather.

Designed for two, discover the serene beauty of San Francisco’s waterways with a self-guided kayaking experience at either Lake Merced or China Basin. Enrollment includes the rental of a tandem (two-person) kayak and covers up to two participants. Personal flotation devices are provided for all participants. Level 4 - This class should improve participants' proficiency in performing the swimming strokes that were introduced in Level 3. Participants perform these strokes with increased proficiency and can swim them for greater distances. In addition, participants learn the arm actions that accompany the scissors kick and breaststroke kick to begin performing the sidestroke and breaststroke at rudimentary proficiency levels. Participants also learn the back crawl and butterfly at rudimentary proficiency levels, in addition to the basics of performing a simple open turn at a wall. Prerequisite: Successful completion of Level 3.

Level 3 - This class builds on the skills learned in Levels 1 and 2. Participants learn to swim the front crawl and elementary backstroke at rudimentary proficiency levels and are introduced to the scissors and dolphin kicks. Participants learn the survival float and increase the time duration for treading water. Participants also learn the rules for headfirst entries and begin to learn to enter the water headfirst from a seated position at poolside (if the water is 9 feet deep or deeper). On successful completion of Level 3, participants have achieved basic water competency in a pool environment. Prerequisite: Successful completion of Level 2. Children must be 48 to participate.

All levels are welcome.

The Maunupau    Method-Learning & Fun Tennis System is a curriculum based progressive    learning methodology whose platform begins with a technically themed lecture,    drills to support the lecture, & fun games. Students can expect to learn    the etymology of grips; continental, eastern, semi-western, &    western(full), stances; square, closed, & open,  the difference between topspin &    underspin, pronation on the serve, ground strokes & volleys, match play,    & the art of the rally. Campers will also learn SAT vocabulary words, a    tennis themed art project, & chess. Tennis is a high cerebral process    game & all of the other activities of the camp aide in this decision    making process.  Campers are never    under any pressure to perform, only to learn, & to conduct themselves    with sportspersonship on court.

Level 3 - This class builds on the skills learned in Levels 1 and 2. Participants learn to swim the front crawl and elementary backstroke at rudimentary proficiency levels and are introduced to the scissors and dolphin kicks. Participants learn the survival float and increase the time duration for treading water. Participants also learn the rules for headfirst entries and begin to learn to enter the water headfirst from a seated position at poolside (if the water is 9 feet deep or deeper). On successful completion of Level 3, participants have achieved basic water competency in a pool environment. Prerequisite: Successful completion of Level 2. Children must be 48 to participate.

event_name: Ropes Course Outdoor Experience - Summer Camp- Ages 13-16
event_description: Designed for teens    ready to push their limits in a supportive outdoor setting, this camp blends    challenge, teamwork, and leadership development on the McLaren Park Ropes    Course. Each day introduces new goals—starting with low-element initiatives    that build trust and communication, and progressing to high-climbing    challenges that test focus and determination.                        Campers will experience signature elements such as the Pyramids, Vertical    Playpen, or Leap of Faith—all with full safety equipment and trained staff    support. Between ropes-course sessions, campers will enjoy guided hikes    through McLaren Park and a variety of field games that promote teamwork,    leadership, and fun. The week will also include an off-site field trip, giving    campers a chance to celebrate their accomplishments and connect beyond the    ropes course.                        This camp is fully outdoors and does not offer extended care.

In    this beginner class, each student can expect to design and fabricate a unique    sterling silver stone set ring. Students will learn foundational    silversmithing techniques- cut metal with the jewelers saw, sand, file,    stamp, form, solder and create a bezel cup in order to fabricate their final    product. This class is a great opportunity to learn a new skill or brush up    on existing metalsmithing skills. All levels of experience are welcome.    Materials and tools are provided with the cost of the class, but please feel    free to bring your own cabochons and supplies. If this will be your first    ring and you would like to purchase your own cabochons to use in the class,    please purchase oval or circular. The Jewelry Studio is located on the upper    floor, with stair access only. For accommodations requests or accessibility    questions, please contact rpdaccess@sfgov.org or 415-831-6841. Please note    that submitting your request at least two weeks before the beginning of the    class will help ensure availability.

event_name: New Technologies: Electronic Inventor Lab with AI Coding
event_description: What if    you could invent the machine to feed your dog when it's hungry, or the    clothing that lights up to the rhythms of your heart beat? In this hands-on    course we’ll use AI to do the coding while we focus on learning to use    programmable microcontrollers (Arduinos) to interface with the physical world    through sensors, animating LED displays, motors, and more. Moving from group    projects to individual inventions, you will learn the principles and theory    of practical electronics and circuit building, as well as best practices for    creating AI generated code that can enable your creative power. Learn how to    get your ideas and inventions out of your head and into existence. No    experience is required. Basic electronics supplies are provided for the    class, but there’s an additional material fee of $40. A paid subscription to    an AI service such as ChatGPT or Gemini is recommended. Repeat students are    welcomed and can work on their ongoing projects. This class pairs well with a    Digital Fab Lab course that focuses on building and fabrication.

event_name: Jewelry Workshop: Creating Script with Metal
event_description: For  those who adore typeface and linework-- this class invites you to turn your  love of shapes and worlds into something you can wear daily. Learn to saw  delicate designs that echo hand drawn script or drawings, to solder jump  rings, and to drill holes, shape and polish brass and copper with a Flex  shaft tool. Each piece becomes a small act of translation of your artistic  voice into a luminous physical form! Open to all experience levels, from  those new to metal working as well as those more advanced and looking to  practice creating more detail in their pieces. Tools and materials provided.  The Jewelry Studio is located on the upper floor, with stair access only. For  accommodations requests or accessibility questions, please contact  rpdaccess@sfgov.org or 415-831-6841. Please note that submitting your request  at least two weeks before the beginning of the class will help ensure  availability.

event_name: Aquatics - Learn to Swim - MLK Pool
event_description: Level 1 This class is designed to orient participants to the aquatic environment and to help them gain basic aquatic skills. In addition, participants start learning about how to be safe around water. Children must be 48 to participate.Level 2 This class builds on the basic aquatic skills and water safety skills and concepts learned in level 1. Participants begin to perform skills at a slightly more advanced level and begin gaining rudimentary propulsive skills on both the front and the back. This level marks the beginning of independent aquatic locomotion skills. Prerequisite- Completion of Level 1. Children must be 48 to participate.Level 3 This class builds on the skills learned in Levels 1 and 2. Participants learn to swim the front crawl and elementary backstroke at rudimentary proficiency levels and are introduced to the scissors and dolphin kicks. Participants learn the survival float and increase the time duration for treading water. Participants also learn the rules for headfirst entries and begin to learn to enter the water headfirst from a seated position at poolside (if the water is 9 feet deep or deeper). On successful completion of Level 3, participants have achieved basic water competency in a pool environment. Prerequisite: Successful completion of Level 2. Children must be 48 to participate.Level 4 This class should improve participants' proficiency in performing the swimming strokes that were introduced in Level 3. Participants perform these strokes with increased proficiency and can swim them for greater distances. In this fast-paced, challenge-packed adventure, fully supervised teams of teens race across San Francisco using public transit, street maps, and sharp teamwork to uncover clues hidden at recreation centers throughout the city. Every day brings a new mission—mini-games, mystery puzzles, sports challenges, art tasks, community activities, and neighborhood photo quests—each designed to spark creativity, curiosity, and collaboration. Whether you’re athletic, artistic, brainy, or boldly adventurous, there’s a challenge waiting for you. The week builds toward an epic, staff-guided final race across the city, where campers put their navigation skills, problem-solving, and team spirit to the ultimate test!

event_name: Aquatics - Learn to Swim - North Beach Pool
event_description: Level 1 This class is designed to orient participants to the aquatic environment and to help them gain basic aquatic skills. In addition, participants start learning about how to be safe around water. Children must be 48 to participate.Level 2 This class builds on the basic aquatic skills and water safety skills and concepts learned in level 1. Participants begin to perform skills at a slightly more advanced level and begin gaining rudimentary propulsive skills on both the front and the back. This level marks the beginning of independent aquatic locomotion skills. Prerequisite- Completion of Level 1. Children must be 48 to participate.Level 3 This class builds on the skills learned in Levels 1 and 2. Participants learn to swim the front crawl and elementary backstroke at rudimentary proficiency levels and are introduced to the scissors and dolphin kicks. Participants learn the survival float and increase the time duration for treading water. Participants also learn the rules for headfirst entries and begin to learn to enter the water headfirst from a seated position at poolside (if the water is 9 feet deep or deeper). On successful completion of Level 3, participants have achieved basic water competency in a pool environment. Prerequisite: Successful completion of Level 2. Children must be 48 to participate.Level 4 This class should improve participants' proficiency in performing the swimming strokes that were introduced in Level 3. Participants perform these strokes with increased proficiency and can swim them for greater distances. In addition, participants learn the arm actions that accompany the scissors kick and breaststroke kick to begin performing the sidestroke and breaststroke at rudimentary proficiency levels. Participants also learn the back crawl and butterfly at rudimentary proficiency levels, in addition to the basics of performing a simple open turn at a wall. Prerequisite: Successful completion of Level 3.Level 5 This class focuses on helping participants refine their performance of all six swimming strokes (front crawl, back crawl, butterfly, breaststroke, elementary backstroke, and sidestroke). Participants perform these strokes with increased proficiency and can swim them for greater distances. Participants also learn to perform flip turns on the front and back. Prerequisite: Successful completion of Level 4.Registration is limited to two sessions per person.

Are you    retired and looking for something new to explore?  Do you have time on a Tuesday afternoon and    are curious about trying drawing and painting in a fun, laid back    environment? Are you wanting to connect with your intuition, play, or    destress using art as a vehicle?     Playful Intuitive Art Exploration is a fun and exploratory drawing and    painting class that gives folks an opportunity to express themselves    creatively through creating art and connecting with their intuition. We will    be taking inspiration from unique real-world images (centered around nature    and microscopic photography) which incorporate interesting colors, textures,    and shapes. You will learn basic drawing techniques using the elements of art    and will be experimenting with abstract and representational concepts using    your intuition as a guide. Level 4 - This class should improve participants' proficiency in performing the swimming strokes that were introduced in Level 3. Participants perform these strokes with increased proficiency and can swim them for greater distances. In addition, participants learn the arm actions that accompany the scissors kick and breaststroke kick to begin performing the sidestroke and breaststroke at rudimentary proficiency levels. Participants also learn the back crawl and butterfly at rudimentary proficiency levels, in addition to the basics of performing a simple open turn at a wall. Prerequisite: Successful completion of Level 3.

Activities include hikes and nature activities, games, campfire safety, arts and crafts, legends storytelling and rock climbing. Camp hours are from 9am-3:30pm, except for Thursdays. Each Thursday, families are invited to a performance of songs and skits from 4pm-5pm. On Thursdays, campers also enjoy a campfire lunch that includes hot dogs and s'mores. Extended care hours are included in the camp fee from 8:30am-9:00am for drop off. Pick up is no later than 5:30pm. Parents will need to walk to the Silver Tree building every day for pick up (about a 10 minute walk into the Canyon).

event_name: City Outdoor Rec Expedition - Girls - Session 2
event_description: Adventure awaits—starting in San Francisco’s parks and ending beneath the stars at Camp Mather.  Over two memorable weeks, campers join CORE (City Outdoor Rec Expedition) for Outdoor Rec favorites like rock climbing, archery, kayaking, and exploring everything the city has to offer—all while learning the basics of tent setup, outdoor living, and caring for the environments they explore. Then the journey continues to Camp Mather, where campers trade city sights for mountain air, lakeside fun, and classic camp traditions—campfires, hiking, ropes course challenges, and more. We’ll travel by bus on Monday and spend 4 nights under the starts, returning on Friday. This fully outdoor camp encourages participants to grow in confidence, build new friendships, and deepen their appreciation for nature—whether they’re adventuring close to home or miles from the city. A mandatory pre-camp meeting will be held prior to the start of Week 2 to review packing lists, logistics, and expectations for the overnight week at Camp Mather.

Come to a  place where we build Deaf community, language and culture. The    Deaf Club will be meeting once a month on the first Friday of each month    during the season. This space provides time for Deaf community members and their    families, as well as anyone interested in engaging in Deaf culture and    developing American Sign Language skills.  The first    half of the evening is designed to support those learning American Sign    Language (ASL), including ASL students, and parents who have a child who is    Deaf or hard of hearing.  The second half of the evening is focused on    providing a space for members of the Deaf community, to share culture,    resources and socialize in a sign language-only environment.  Anyone is    welcome to stay for the second half of the evening; but they should be    prepared to hold their own in conversation.  This is a drop-in program,    but a Recreation and Parks Play Pass or sign-in is required.

Calling    all young explorers! Join us for a night of outdoor fun at Glen Park Rec    Center. Designed for ages 8-12, this exciting overnight adventure will teach    camping basics like pitching a tent (with tents provided), cooking a    camping-style dinner, and sleeping under the stars (indoors, of course!).    We’ll also hit the trails for a canyon hike and try some indoor rock climbing    with all gear provided. Our certified staff will lead the way, ensuring a    safe and fun experience. Snacks and dinner are provided—just bring your    adventure spirit!

Whether you’re athletic, artistic, brainy, or boldly adventurous, there’s a challenge waiting for you. Join    The Call of the Sea, St. Francis Yacht Club, and other youth,      in a three-hour voyage on Seaward, an 82-foot ship. All youth      will become sailors and scientists for the day. You will get to      help sail the ship. You will also get to learn about ecology,      marine life, sailing skills and teamwork. Seaward will do a lap      of the bay. You will board at the docks of the St. Francis Yacht      Club. The route will begin with a ride to the Golden Gate Bridge,      a view from below the bridge and then will go around Angel      Island, near Alcatraz and then, on the way back to the yacht      club, you will sail past city landmarks along the waterfront.      Every participant should bring non- skid shoes such as tennis      shoes, long pants, a jacket and layers of clothes. The Saint      Francis Yacht Club will only be a pick up/drop off point for this      class. There is no waiting area for parents or guardians. The      address is 99 Yacht Rd, San Francisco, CA 94123. This event will      go on rain or shine. Questions please contact Rex.Biteng@sfgov.      org **EACH CHILD MAY SIGN UP FOR ONLY ONE SESSION.

Level 3 - This class builds on the skills learned in Levels 1 and 2. Participants learn to swim the front crawl and elementary backstroke at rudimentary proficiency levels and are introduced to the scissors and dolphin kicks. Participants learn the survival float and increase the time duration for treading water. Participants also learn the rules for headfirst entries and begin to learn to enter the water headfirst from a seated position at poolside (if the water is 9 feet deep or deeper). On successful completion of Level 3, participants have achieved basic water competency in a pool environment. Prerequisite: Successful completion of Level 2. Children must be 48 to participate.

SLICE is a community-focused program for teens and transition-aged youth with Autism or other developmental disabilities, ages 15–25. Designed to support growing independence, SLICE offers opportunities for young adults to engage in meaningful community service, develop professional and life skills, and participate in collaborative projects with other TR camps. Participants practice social skills, team building, and community integration through hands-on activities and local field trips, including those planned with the Everybody Travels Camp.  Due to a low staff ratio, participants must be independent with personal care, able to stay with the group with verbal prompting, and prepared for walking and travel during outings. For more information, call 415-831-6841 or email RPDTR@sfgov.org.

event_name: New Technologies: Laser Cut Animals, Heroes, and More!

Join    The Call of the Sea, St. Francis Yacht Club, and other youth,      in a three-hour voyage on Seaward, an 82-foot ship. All youth      will become sailors and scientists for the day. You will get to      help sail the ship. You will also get to learn about ecology,      marine life, sailing skills and teamwork. Seaward will do a lap      of the bay. You will board at the docks of the St. Francis Yacht      Club. The route will begin with a ride to the Golden Gate Bridge,      a view from below the bridge and then will go around Angel      Island, near Alcatraz and then, on the way back to the yacht      club, you will sail past city landmarks along the waterfront.      Every participant should bring non- skid shoes such as tennis      shoes, long pants, a jacket and layers of clothes. The Saint      Francis Yacht Club will only be a pick up/drop off point for this      class. There is no waiting area for parents or guardians. The      address is 99 Yacht Rd, San Francisco, CA 94123. This event will      go on rain or shine. Questions please contact Rex.Biteng@sfgov.      org **EACH CHILD MAY SIGN UP FOR ONLY ONE SESSION.

Our after school program is an experience that provides a safe and nurturing environment for youth in grades K-5 to develop life long recreation and leisure skills. Participants will receive homework assistance, be exposed to an environment that will promote literacy, encourage communication and empowerment skills while providing recreation activities that include sports, group games, arts and culture, community exploration and seasonal special events. Parents will have the option to pay the entire fee upon registration. Payment plans are available. $270 is due upon registration and on the first day of October, January and March of the school year.

This    class introduces hand building techniques including pinch, coil, and slab    construction, and basic wheel throwing techniques of wedging, centering,    throwing cups and bowls, and trimming. Clay is available for purchase at the studio with a    100-pound clay limit per session. No outside clay or glazes allowed. Students    must purchase a basic pottery tool kit prior to the first day of class.    Suggested materials list can be found at sharonartstudio.org. Students may    enroll in only one ceramics class per session. Note: New students (new to    ceramics or Sharon Art Studio) must attend the first three classes. All skill    levels welcome.

Level 3 - This class builds on the skills learned in Levels 1 and 2. Participants learn to swim the front crawl and elementary backstroke at rudimentary proficiency levels and are introduced to the scissors and dolphin kicks. Participants learn the survival float and increase the time duration for treading water. Participants also learn the rules for headfirst entries and begin to learn to enter the water headfirst from a seated position at poolside (if the water is 9 feet deep or deeper). On successful completion of Level 3, participants have achieved basic water competency in a pool environment. Prerequisite: Successful completion of Level 2. Children must be 48 to participate.

This    2-day workshop serves as a hands-on introduction to this demanding but    beautiful photographic process, invented by Frederick Scott Archer in the    mid-19th Century. Participants will use large format period-correct cameras    to explore the craftsmanship of tintypes and ambrotypes. The goal is to    provide a taste of how the process works, with each participant walking away    with 3-4 plates. We will be making photographs at/around the Harvey Milk    Photo Center. Participants are invited to bring their own props, old    costumes, etc. Note: Please bring aprons and work clothing (materials used    can easily stain clothing). This course is taught by Allan Barnes, a former    photojournalist who has been working extensively with the process for almost    ten years.

event_name: Tennis - Vocabulary, Chess & Art Spring Break Camp
event_description: The Maunupau    Method-Learning & Fun Tennis System is an eclectic mix of tennis    instruction methodologies from; SFRPD, USTA, USPTA, PTR, & the MM-LAFTS    certification curriculum, as well as 30+ years of on court coaching in the    public & private sector. Instruction is divided into; safety &    technical lecture, drills to support the theme of the technical lecture, fun    games that are related to the theme of the technical lecture, & match    play. Campers will learn the etymology of tennis grips; continental, eastern    forehand, semi-western, full western, & eastern 1 handed backhand.    Students will also learn stances; square, open, & closed, as well as    pronation on the serve. Campers will also be introduced to the game of chess,    a tennis themed art project, & a word of the day, to increase high school    vocabulary.

The Silver Tree    Leaders In Training (L.I.T.) is located in Glen Canyon Park. Participants    must be between the ages of 12 to 14 years old. The LIT program is a 2-week    camp experience introducing young teens to leadership, assisting Silver Tree    staff with camper mentorship, and developing teamwork skills in the hopes of    preparing LITs to become staff in the summer camp setting. LITs will also    participate in a variety of team building exercises, campfire building, basic    wilderness safety, rock climbing, and will be lightly incorporated into    traditional Silver Tree activities. Camp hours are from 9am-4pm. Parents will    need to walk to the Silver Tree building     for the first day of camp, which is a 10-minute walk into Glen Canyon. Registration for this camp is limited to one session per person.

event_name: Lifeguard Training Class with Oxygen Administration
event_description: **This class has been updated and a new fee has been added**  The primary purpose of the lifeguard training program is to provide entry level lifeguard skills and knowledge to prevent, recognize, and respond to emergencies and to provide care for sudden illnesses until advanced medical personnel arrives and takes over. This program includes a lifeguard training course with aquatics specific first aid and cardio-pulmonary resuscitation (CPR) training. NOTE: Participants MUST pass the pre-test on the first day of class. The test consists of the following: 300-yard swim; tread water for 2 minutes without the use of arms; swim 20 yards, then surface dive to retrieve a 10-lb. brick, swim back to the starting point with both hands on the brick, and climb out of the water without assistance, skill must be performed in under 1minute 40 seconds. Depending on the skills of the participants and their comprehension of the materials presented, the participants may be dismissed early or need to stay later each day.

event_description: Designed for teens    ready to push their limits in a supportive outdoor setting, this camp blends    challenge, teamwork, and leadership development on the McLaren Park Ropes    Course. This camp is fully outdoors and does not offer extended care.

The course features high elements designed to help participants stretch beyond their comfort zones, all while safely harnessed and belayed by our trained staff. event_name: Drawing at the de Young Museum
event_description: This hands-on workshop invites participants to explore the rich, tactile world of oil pastels through a series of guided exercises inspired by the vibrant collections of the de Young Museum. Blending drawing and painting techniques, participants will learn how to layer, blend, and build texture using oil pastels to create expressive, colorful works on paper. Participants will engage with museum artworks as sources of inspiration—drawing connections between historical and contemporary uses of color, composition, and gesture. Emphasis will be placed on personal expression, process, and experimentation rather than representational accuracy, making this workshop accessible to all skill levels.At the de Young Museum, sketching is allowed in the permanent collection with a pencil and a small (9 x 12 inch or smaller) pad. At the first class the class will meet at the lily pound outside of the de Young. Please do not be late for the first day of the class as important information and rules will be covered before going inside the museum. Students are responsible for their own entry tickets if needed. Please bring drawing materials to class. See materials list at sharonartstudio.org.

Participants also learn the rules for headfirst entries and begin to learn to enter the water headfirst from a seated position at poolside (if the water is 9 feet deep or deeper). On successful completion of Level 3, participants have achieved basic water competency in a pool environment. Prerequisite: Successful completion of Level 2. Children must be 48 to participate.

At the Kezar Flag Football Camp, players dive into the core fundamentals of the sport while building a strong understanding of game strategy and teamwork. Campers take part in energetic drills, skill-focused games, and exciting scrimmages designed to boost confidence and ability. With an emphasis on fun, development, and guidance from our enthusiastic, experienced coaches, participants sharpen their skills all summer long. Catch your first wave at Mirada Surf Beach in Half Moon Bay! This beginner-friendly surfing class is ideal for first-timers or those looking to build confidence in the water. Learn the basics of paddling, popping up, and ocean safety from experienced instructors in a fun, supportive environment. Instruction includes beach-based practice and supervised time in the water. Surfboards are provided. Note: Participants must be comfortable swimming in the ocean and pass a required deep-water swim test. Waiver and swim test forms are required. Level 4 - This class should improve participants' proficiency in performing the swimming strokes that were introduced in Level 3. Participants perform these strokes with increased proficiency and can swim them for greater distances. In addition, participants learn the arm actions that accompany the scissors kick and breaststroke kick to begin performing the sidestroke and breaststroke at rudimentary proficiency levels. Participants also learn the back crawl and butterfly at rudimentary proficiency levels, in addition to the basics of performing a simple open turn at a wall. Prerequisite: Successful completion of Level 3.

event_name: Woodworking for Tweens: From the Animal Room to the Woodshop
event_description: **The start time for this class has been updated to starting at 4pm**Bat    houses, bird houses, and bee houses, oh my! How can we bring our knowledge of    local ecology into the woodshop? We'll start this session with our Animal    Room staff, learning how we can be better neighbors to our local species.    Then we'll move into the woodshop, and using the drill press, bandsaw, and a    range of hand tools, we'll design and build small structures to support local    wildlife.

There is much more in Hatha yoga practice than poses. In this series, we still explore practices aimed to improve our physical well-being through asanas (poses); we also introduce practices that include pranayama (breath expansion) techniques, and finally we experience the benefits of simple meditation techniques. The intention is awareness at a physical, mental and emotional level on as well as off the mat.

A San Francisco tradition, Silver Tree Day Camp is located in Glen Park Canyon. Silver Tree is a 1-week camp for kids who enjoy the outdoors, being in nature, and don’t mind getting dirty. Activities include hikes and nature activities, games, campfire safety, arts and crafts, legends storytelling and rock climbing. Camp hours are from 9am-3:30pm, except for Thursdays. Each Thursday, families are invited to a performance of songs and skits from 4pm-5pm. On Thursdays, campers also enjoy a campfire lunch that includes hot dogs and s'mores. Extended care hours are included in the camp fee from 8:30am-9:00am for drop off. Pick up is no later than 5:30pm. Parents will need to walk to the Silver Tree building every day for pick up (about a 10 minute walk into the Canyon).

Designed for two, discover the serene beauty of San Francisco’s waterways with a self-guided kayaking experience at either Lake Merced or China Basin. Paddle at your own pace as you explore the lake’s tranquil waters, diverse wildlife, and natural habitats; an ideal way to connect with nature and enjoy a refreshing outdoor adventure right in the city. Enrollment includes the rental of a tandem (two-person) kayak and covers up to two participants. Personal flotation devices are provided for all participants. Children 12 and under must be accompanied by an adult in the kayak. All participants must sign a release waiver on the day of their session. Instructional safety videos must be viewed prior to paddling; a link to the videos and waiver will be emailed the week before your scheduled time. Registered participants under the age of 12 must be accompanied on the water by a registered parent or guardian. Expect to get wet—please bring a towel and a change of clothes.

Our after school program is an experience that provides a safe and nurturing environment for youth in grades K-5 to develop life long recreation and leisure skills. Participants will receive homework assistance, be exposed to an environment that will promote literacy, encourage communication and empowerment skills while providing recreation activities that include sports, group games, arts and culture, community exploration and seasonal special events. All staff and volunteers receive background checks and are trained in safety skills. Start times may vary based on the school release time. The fee for the school year is $1080. Parents will have the option to pay the entire fee upon registration. Payment plans are available. $270.00 is due upon registration and on the first day of October, January and March of the school year.
